title: Signal Threshold Field
prompt:
Create a production-ready Signal Threshold Field using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original numeric setting lets a person choose a review threshold from Low, Standard, or High. Three clearly named native buttons update a compact readout and move an orange indicator across a solid track. The field must state the selected threshold in text, not only through the indicator position. Use a light Bento composition with #F4F5F6 stage, white card, #ECECEE borders, 22px corners, a gentle neutral shadow, #111111 primary type, and #4A4A4F support copy. Orange is the only bright accent; use solid fills and no gradients.

Use a labelled group of 44px native buttons with aria-pressed and an aria-live polite status. Click, Enter, and Space must select a threshold. Move the indicator with a spring using stiffness 320 and damping 26, fade the explanatory sentence over 150ms ease-out, and avoid any perpetual animation. Keep component data local and deterministic: no timers, random values, storage, remote assets, network calls, layout reads, or browser-global dependencies. Favor transform and opacity.

At 320px, controls must remain legible without horizontal overflow; at a large detail preview, allow a maximum width that keeps the setting compact. Include a visible 3px focus ring, logical tab order, AA contrast, and redundant text and shape cues. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by snapping the indicator while keeping a short opacity fade. Return complete paste-ready client code, runnable pure CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML: <fieldset class="signal-threshold"><button aria-pressed="true">Standard</button></fieldset> */
.signal-threshold{max-width:340px;padding:20px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:22px;background:#fff}.signal-threshold button{min-height:44px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:12px;background:#fff;font-weight:800}.signal-threshold button[aria-pressed=true]{border-color:#ff9950;box-shadow:inset 0 -3px #ff9950}.signal-threshold button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.signal-threshold *{transition:none}}

demoCode:
